title: Big Mom’s Broken Memory
description: On pages 11–14, this part focuses on Unknown person #9, Giants, Big Mom, Unknown person #10, Unknown person #11, Unknown person #12 as Big Mom’s past explains the emotional weakness that the assassination plan tries to exploit.
scene: Big Mom’s childhood reveals the loss and confusion behind her obsession with Mother Carmel.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: It's nearing 40 years! Have you ever heard of a duel that takes that long?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I feel ya! I say we give 'em 10 more years, and then go get 'em!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The great warriors of the land took Linlin in with tolerance and generosity.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But if you just bear that for a little, you get to enjoy the festival!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: One day, either Dorry or Broggy will return!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The only thing that can pass your lips is water!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: You are a warrior! Keep fighting, hajrudin!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #9
dialogue.text: Are you and your friends ready?

dialogue.speaker: Big Mom
dialogue.text: Ah! Isn't this your first time, Linlin?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: in Elbaf, until the winter solstice festival, we fast! For 12 days!

dialogue.speaker: Giants
dialogue.text: They will revive the giant warrior pirates!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Zabababa! I like that spirit of yours, Hajrudin!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: caramel and her talk of trade are all well and good,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But we must forget the way of the warrior!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Former capians of the giant warrior pirates (oldest warriors in the world) heroes of the giant peoples

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Why are the two of you here in town today?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We've come to call the children of the town. It's time to eat Semla!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Right before the fast, we eat selam to get in our nutrients!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They stuff a sweet dough made with marzipan with whipped cream

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: They're so cool! All giantfolk respect those two warriors!

dialogue.speaker: Big Mom
dialogue.text: Good to see you here, Linlin.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And cover the top with powdered sugar! It's our final treat before the fast!()

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Are we gonna eat it now?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: For Prince Luki, who was born just the other day in the castle!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: And for ROLD! Gold! And BALG!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We thank the Sun for the growth of our children!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: We've got plenty, so eat up!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You can't eat them at that speed! We're not going to have enough to go around!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: The third day of the fast

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: mother! I'm so hungry I feel like I'm going to die.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The winter solstice festival celebrates the death and rebirth of the sun.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The sun will pass through the harsh winter,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And will once again warm us with its benevolent light.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The deeper your gratitude to the sun will be.

dialogue.speaker: Charlotte Linlin
dialogue.text: If Mother says so, I will... try.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Linlin had come to love mother with all her heart.

summary: This section advances the Whole Cake Island Tea Party storyline with 85 dialogue beats. Big Mom’s past reframes her weakness and the emotional core of the failed assassination plan.
